seed: longing vs resolve; repair_vs_damage; mirror_broken; pulse; barcode against broken_dots; lit_edges; contained_vs_overflowing; thread; serenity_urgency | abstract variation request 196; target batch count 500
title: Echoes of Tension
reasoning: The emotional tone revolves around the tension between longing and resolve, embodied through a palette that shifts from muted tones to sharper contrasts. The quiet pressure of colors like off-white and dust-gray evokes a sense of calm and yearning, while the compressed red adds urgency against a dark navy backdrop, drawing the eye to the center as a collision point that emphasizes the emotional struggle. The top half of the square, representing longing, features flowing tide patterns with broken pixels indicating unresolved feelings. The bottom half, representing resolve, adopts a static approach, with solid blocks reinforcing strength. This juxtaposition captures the essence of the seed: the intertwining of desires and boundaries, with negative space allowing the emotional weight to breathe, suggesting both serenity and urgency in a cohesive abstract narrative.

layout.name: split_horizontal
layout.description: Top 4 rows are emotion A, bottom 4 rows are emotion B.

composition.name: near_vs_far
composition.idea: Close paired marks are separated from distant echoes by a wide quiet field.

referenceExample.title: Resilient Scar
referenceExample.pattern: thin broken structure crossed by a repaired vertical path

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
